snapflood.name: SnapFlood
snapflood.summary: A citizen-science platform that turns everyday smartphones into a real-time, global flood-observation network — building the fine-grained data urban flood science has always lacked.
snapflood.description: Traditional flood monitoring works for rivers but struggles with highly variable city floods, which lack fine-resolution rainfall and flood-flow data. SnapFlood lets anyone capture and upload geotagged flood photos and videos with simple metadata, growing a living archive that feeds research, modelling and faster municipal response.

features.title: Capture & upload
features.description: Photos and videos showing flooded areas, water levels and impacts.

features.title: Rich metadata
features.description: Add city, area, water-depth estimate, rainfall level and remarks.

features.title: Automatic geotagging
features.description: Location sharing tags every submission to a precise point.

features.title: Global coverage
features.description: Contribute flood observations from anywhere in the world.

features.title: Real-time archive
features.description: Every report adds to a growing, research-ready flood database.

features.title: Free & lightweight
features.description: A fast, free mobile app — no specialist hardware required.

howItWorks.title: Capture
howItWorks.description: Citizens submit geotagged photos/videos with metadata via the app or a Google Form.

howItWorks.title: Aggregate
howItWorks.description: Submissions flow into a real-time, global flood-data archive.

howItWorks.title: Augment
howItWorks.description: Additional flood signals are extracted from social media (X/Twitter).

howItWorks.title: Feed UFIS
howItWorks.description: The archive feeds the Urban Flood Information System’s forecasting and modelling.

howItWorks.title: Act
howItWorks.description: Outputs support research, engineering, policy and faster municipal response.

snapflood.funding: Originated from a Government of India DST-SPLICE (Climate Change Programme) funded project, with ongoing support from the AI CoE for Sustainable Cities.

varshamitra.name: VarshaAnalytics
varshamitra.summary: RAFT’s rainfall-analytics product — turning station, satellite and model rainfall into clear, actionable insight for communities and decision-makers.
varshamitra.description: VarshaAnalytics grew from VarshaMitra — IIT Hyderabad’s 2023 community mascot (Varsha = rain, Mitra = friend) that championed crowd-sourced flood reporting for SnapFlood. Today it brings station, radar, satellite and model rainfall together into a single, easy-to-read view, helping communities and decision-makers understand and act on rainfall and flood risk.

features.title: Rainfall monitoring
features.description: A unified view of station, radar and satellite rainfall.

roadmap.name: UFIS Public Dashboard
roadmap.tagline: City-scale flood information, in one place
roadmap.summary: A public interface to the Urban Flood Information System — forecasts, depths and advisories for the city.

roadmap.name: Flood Early-Warning Service
roadmap.summary: Targeted, impact-based flood alerts delivered to agencies and citizens before water rises.

roadmap.name: Rainfall Nowcast API
roadmap.tagline: Radar + AI short-range rainfall, on tap
roadmap.summary: Programmatic access to deep-learning rainfall nowcasts derived from radar reflectivity.
